notes: Production runtime intentionally remains CC7.5.25 legacy layered loader.
AdminKit Core is still side-by-side and not active in production webhook routing.
/debug/core-canary-send can manually send a Core screen only to an explicit adminId and still remains gated by ADMINKIT_CORE_SEND_ENABLED plus ADMINKIT_CORE_CANARY_ADMINS or ADMINKIT_CORE_CANARY_ALL.
Manual send requires adminId for real sending; dryRun=1 can be used without real delivery.
The canary webhook is isolated from legacy bot.js and does not fallback to legacy handlers.
The canary webhook path is not auto-registered in MAX; production webhook registration still uses the legacy webhook path.
Optional extra access token can be added through ADMINKIT_CORE_WEBHOOK_TOKEN for direct canary webhook calls.
/debug/core-canary-preview creates a dry-run update and shows route, Core screen, and strict MAX payload without sending messages.
The delivery adapter removes Core metadata from outbound inline_keyboard payload and keeps only payload.buttons for MAX API compatibility.
Comments identity/open-state core remains unchanged from accepted CC7.5.6.
Buttons manager remains unchanged from accepted CC7.5.16+.
Lead magnet flow remains from CC7.5.24, including Start reset, URL material handling and step 4 to 5 bottom-menu cleanup.
Main menu hard root points to adminkit-admin-flows-7525.
Visible lead-magnet wording is patched before legacy/base admin-flow modules are imported, so old labels like 'Добавить новый подарок' should not appear.
No overlay, no floating hints, no public/app.js override, no external code.run /app comments button.
Next migration step: verify /debug/core-canary-send?route=main.home&dryRun=1 after redeploy, then enable Core real send only for one canary admin.
